title: Ixora crebrifolia

intro: Ixora crebrifolia, also known as Jungle Geranium or Wild Jasmine, is a flowering plant native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ixora crebrifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Ixora crebrifolia is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote digestion and reduce inflammation.
